step_1_context.text: If I had more time, I would travel around the world. But I am too busy with work right now.

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: Use second conditional for unreal or unlikely situations in the present or future.
step_2_explanation.formula: If + past simple, would + base verb
step_2_explanation.common_mistake: Do not use 'would' in the if-clause. Say 'If I had' not 'If I would have'.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: If she ___ rich, she would buy a house by the sea.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: In second conditional, use 'were' for all subjects (formal) or 'was' (informal). 'Were' is the standard form.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: What would you do if you ___ the lottery?
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: The if-clause uses past simple. 'Won' is the past simple of 'win'.

step_3_interactive_exercises.incorrect_sentence: If I would know the answer, I would tell you.
step_3_interactive_exercises.corrected_sentence: If I knew the answer, I would tell you.
step_3_interactive_exercises.error_analysis: Never use 'would' in the if-clause. Use past simple: knew.

step_3_interactive_exercises.sentence: If he ___ (speak) Spanish, he could work in Madrid.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Past simple of 'speak' is 'spoke'. This describes an unreal present situation.
